jokes.text: It's 4:30 in the morning. Do you know where your kids are? If you are Ryan's parents or Pam's parents or my parents, you do. They're gonna be in this van. With me. Who am I? Nothing to fear. I am just a 44-year-old guy with a paper route.
jokes.setup: Michael opens with ominous 'do you know where your kids are' PSA format
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just a middle-aged man with a paper route, making the dramatic setup absurd
jokes.explanation: Excellent cold open that establishes the premise while subverting PSA expectations. Perfect Michael Scott delusion of grandeur.

jokes.text: Time to make the donuts!
jokes.setup: Michael enthusiastically quotes the old Dunkin' Donuts commercial
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate energy for 4:30 AM paper delivery, shows his misguided enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Michael's misplaced corporate enthusiasm

jokes.text: Come on. No, I promise I won't do it again. Come on.
jokes.setup: Ryan pleading desperately about something unspecified
jokes.punchline: The pathetic begging reveals how low he's fallen
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Ryan's desperation and fall from grace

jokes.text: De-nied!
jokes.setup: Michael responding to Ryan's pleading
jokes.punchline: Theatrical pronunciation of 'denied' shows his petty power trip
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael theatrical delivery that shows his enjoyment of having power over Ryan

jokes.text: Ever since I've gotten clean, there's something about fresh morning air... that just really makes me sick.
jokes.setup: Ryan sets up expectation that clean living and fresh air are positive
jokes.punchline: Subverts by saying the fresh air makes him sick
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that reveals Ryan's shallow recovery and continued negativity

jokes.text: Boner patrol! Arrest that man! Your donuts make me go nuts!
jokes.setup: Michael yelling inappropriate things during early morning deliveries
jokes.punchline: The juvenile and inappropriate nature of his outbursts
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate behavior that's cringe-worthy but fits his character

jokes.text: We got the van at a used car lot. We think it says 'Alleluia Church of Scranton' in Korean. It was either this or an old school bus with an owl living in it.
jokes.setup: Explanation of their delivery van situation
jokes.punchline: The specific detail about the owl living in the school bus
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd detail about the owl that makes the alternative option vivid and ridiculous

jokes.text: You didn't happen to bring any coffee, did you, Michael? - Milk and sugar. - Awesome. You're a life saver.
jokes.setup: Pam asks for coffee, Michael says he brought milk and sugar
jokes.punchline: Sets up the reveal that he literally only brought milk and sugar
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the payoff that Michael literally brought the coffee ingredients separately

jokes.text: Wait, is this just milk and sugar? - That's what I said. Do you drink this every day? Every morning.
jokes.setup: Pam realizes Michael literally brought only milk and sugar
jokes.punchline: Michael confirms he drinks straight milk and sugar every morning
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff that reveals Michael's bizarre morning routine and literal interpretation of coffee ingredients

jokes.text: We have 20... Excuse me. Sorry. Sorry, no. It's... It's a paper company now. It's not for the church.
jokes.setup: Michael taking a business call in the Korean church van
jokes.punchline: Has to explain it's not actually a church anymore, revealing their low-rent operation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff on the church van setup that shows their unprofessional operation

jokes.text: Shame, Jim. I expected more.
jokes.setup: Charles addressing Jim about lost clients
jokes.punchline: The understated disappointment delivered in Charles's dry manner
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Charles's dry disappointment in Jim

jokes.text: Hey, boss. I'd just like to point out that I have been here less time than these guys. - Why are you telling me this?
jokes.setup: Todd trying to deflect blame by pointing out his short tenure
jokes.punchline: Charles's direct question exposes the transparent excuse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Todd's weak attempt to avoid responsibility

jokes.text: I just think the bar should be lower for a newbie. Is this something you really want to have said?
jokes.setup: Todd doubles down on his excuse
jokes.punchline: Charles gives him a chance to reconsider, highlighting how bad the statement is
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Todd's position worse and shows Charles's directness

jokes.text: I don't want to have said that. But I think it's important that you know it.
jokes.setup: Todd acknowledges he shouldn't have said it
jokes.punchline: But then justifies it anyway, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe logic where the character makes it worse while trying to fix it

jokes.text: Been there, done that.
jokes.setup: Angela talks about expense reports and waste
jokes.punchline: Creed's completely inappropriate and mysterious response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - minimal but suggests weird backstory with expense fraud

jokes.text: You know what we need? We need some couches. We should really consider getting a delivery guy. You know what you would love? It's if we built a loft.
jokes.setup: Discussion of practical business needs
jokes.punchline: Ryan's completely impractical loft suggestion
jokes.explanation: Shows Ryan's disconnect from practical business needs and Michael's influence on him

jokes.text: Why would I love that? - Can we afford a delivery guy? - Like in a dorm room. You put your desk underneath, you have your loft up top. You can sleep up top. - I know what a loft is. - Most dorm rooms don't even have that. - Most do in the magazines.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining his loft concept
jokes.punchline: Michael's deadpan 'Most do in the magazines' reveals his source of dorm room knowledge
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation ending with Michael's ridiculous reference to magazine dorm rooms

jokes.text: I feel like I should be thanking you.
jokes.setup: Charles talking to David about his Scranton experience
jokes.punchline: Ironic given how badly things are going
jokes.explanation: Subtle irony showing Charles's corporate speak while everything falls apart

jokes.text: Maybe, and I don't know, if you had just returned Michael's call, none of us would've lost clients.
jokes.setup: David Wallace talking about Michael as a temporary setback
jokes.punchline: Stanley's direct call-out of David's mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley moment - direct, logical criticism delivered with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: Come along, afterthought.
jokes.setup: Charles calling both Jim and Dwight into the meeting
jokes.punchline: Dismissive nickname for Jim shows Charles's clear preference
jokes.explanation: Efficient insult that shows Charles's disdain for Jim in one word

jokes.text: A lame attempt at humor. Swing and a miss.
jokes.setup: Ryan apparently made a joke that fell flat
jokes.punchline: Charles's clinical assessment of the failed humor attempt
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about bad jokes that fits Charles's humorless character perfectly

jokes.text: Why don't you explain what that is... So they can under... - Explain what that is. - Explain what you think that is. - Just explain.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to get Ryan to explain variable cost pricing
jokes.punchline: Multiple failed attempts to hand off the explanation reveal neither understands
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating cringe as both Michael and Ryan try to bluff their way through business knowledge

jokes.text: Ty, I would like you to crunch those numbers again. - It's a program. There's no such... - Just crunch 'em, please. Crunch. Did it help?
jokes.setup: Michael asking to crunch numbers again
jokes.punchline: He literally says 'crunch' as if that would change anything
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment showing Michael's complete lack of business understanding. Highly quotable and perfectly executed.

jokes.text: When a child gets behind the wheel of a car and runs into a tree, you don't blame the child. He didn't know any better. You blame the 30-year-old woman who got in the passenger seat and said, 'Drive, kid. I trust you'.
jokes.setup: Michael creating an analogy about responsibility
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific scenario reveals he's talking about himself and Ryan
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that reveals Michael's twisted logic about his own responsibility while maintaining his self-delusion

jokes.text: Really? Does he do good work or... - No, Jim. I use a bad apiarist.
jokes.setup: Jim asking about Dwight's apiarist
jokes.punchline: Dwight's sarcastic response about obviously using a bad bee expert
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight sarcasm delivered with complete deadpan conviction. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Idiot. Idiot. Idiot. - My new 'Dwight' ring. - I like it. Good, right? Idiot, we're starting back up.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his phone ringtone
jokes.punchline: The ringtone is just Dwight saying 'Idiot' repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to Dwight's frequent insults, showing Jim's playful relationship with the antagonism

jokes.text: Did I ever tell you about the day that Steve Martin died? - Steve Martin's not dead. - I know. But I always thought that the day that he died would be the worst day of my life. And I was wrong. It's this.
jokes.setup: Ryan talking about Steve Martin's death
jokes.punchline: Reveals Martin isn't dead but uses it to dramatize his current situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that reveals Ryan's dramatic self-pity while being genuinely clever about hypothetical grief

jokes.text: I applied to Old Navy, Target and Wal-Mart. None of 'em called me back. Not even for an interview.
jokes.setup: Pam trying to get weekend work for wedding money
jokes.punchline: Can't even get retail interviews, showing how bad things are
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that shows the real consequences of their business failure on a personal level

jokes.text: I never went to Thailand. Really? I went to Fort Lauderdale. Was it nice? Yeah, it was amazing. There was a great pad thai place, though. I love pad thai. You never had pad thai.
jokes.setup: Ryan admitting he lied about Thailand
jokes.punchline: His attempt to maintain the lie through pad thai knowledge fails
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ryan's pathological lying even in small, unnecessary ways

jokes.text: We kidnap the queen, extract her alarm pheromones, place them on a flushable wipe, put that in his bathroom.
jokes.setup: Dwight's elaborate bee-based revenge plan
jokes.punchline: The incredibly specific and absurd methodology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight absurdist plan with specific details that make it both ridiculous and somehow plausible for his character

jokes.text: Oh, man! If only Michael had children. That's how you really apply the pressure. - What is wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Discussion about pressuring Michael
jokes.punchline: Dwight's casual suggestion of threatening children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's shocking but perfectly fits Dwight's amoral business mindset

jokes.text: Seriously? - Are you being serious? - He's bluffing. What you don't understand is that this company's worthl... We don't have...
jokes.setup: Team reacting to buyout offer
jokes.punchline: Ryan almost reveals they're worthless before cutting himself off
jokes.explanation: Good tension moment where Ryan almost blows their negotiating position

jokes.text: We're not only tight-ends, we are also quarterbacks. - Missed the last part. - That's a pun. Got it.
jokes.setup: Michael making a sports metaphor
jokes.punchline: His forced explanation that it's a pun makes it awkward
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael forced wordplay that falls flat, made worse by his need to explain it

jokes.text: I'm really worried I'm gonna say it.
jokes.setup: After multiple reminders not to reveal they're broke
jokes.punchline: Pam's anxiety about accidentally revealing their secret
jokes.explanation: Good psychological comedy showing how repeated warnings create the exact anxiety they're meant to prevent

jokes.text: How the turntables...
jokes.setup: Michael trying to say 'how the tables have turned'
jokes.punchline: Mangles the phrase into 'how the turntables'
jokes.explanation: Iconic Michael malapropism that became a cultural touchstone. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? That is insultingly low. I don't even want to hear what your first offer was. What do you hear?
jokes.setup: Michael rejecting the $12,000 offer
jokes.punchline: David's confusion about what Michael's asking leads to awkward clarification
jokes.explanation: Good miscommunication comedy showing the confusion in high-stakes negotiations

jokes.text: Jerry, the one who got away.
jokes.setup: Michael talking about a lost client
jokes.punchline: Treats a business client like a lost romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's personal attachment to business relationships

jokes.text: May I ask why you're leaving the Michael Scott Paper Company? Really?
jokes.setup: Michael on the phone trying to retain a client
jokes.punchline: The client's response is apparently so obvious it doesn't need stating
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing how obvious their problems are to everyone but them

jokes.text: You don't crack a case. It has a pejorative connotation. Like calling a policeman a cop. You solve a case, and yes, I've solved plenty.
jokes.setup: Dwight correcting Jim's terminology about detective work
jokes.punchline: His pedantic correction and false claim about solving cases
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight pedantry followed by obvious exaggeration about his detective skills

jokes.text: One. Case of the beet bandit. Missing beets from all over the farm. No footprints. Inside job. Mose in socks. Case closed.
jokes.setup: Dwight defending his detective record
jokes.punchline: His one 'case' is absurdly simple farm theft by Mose
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight backstory that's perfectly absurd yet fits his character. Great economy and memorable details.

jokes.text: You two are morons. Got it. Get out.
jokes.setup: Charles dealing with Jim and Dwight's nonsense
jokes.punchline: His direct dismissal and confirmation they understand
jokes.explanation: Efficient character moment showing Charles's no-nonsense management style

jokes.text: How could you do this to me? You just cost me $60,000.
jokes.setup: Ryan blaming others for losing the buyout
jokes.punchline: His selfish focus on personal loss rather than team consequences
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ryan's selfishness and misunderstanding of their situation

jokes.text: Wouldn't you rather have a fishing pole than a fish? I would rather have $60,000, honestly.
jokes.setup: Michael using the fishing metaphor about long-term thinking
jokes.punchline: Ryan's blunt honesty about preferring immediate money
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Michael's business philosophy and Ryan's practical desperation

jokes.text: I want my old job back. I want my old parking space back. I want a Sebring. They don't make them anymore.
jokes.setup: Michael listing his demands
jokes.punchline: Wanting a discontinued car model shows his disconnect from reality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation ending with absurd demand that reveals Michael's impractical thinking

jokes.text: You know, David? I don't care if Ryan murdered his entire family. He is like a son to me.
jokes.setup: Michael defending Ryan despite his past mistakes
jokes.punchline: Extreme hypothetical about family murder shows misplaced loyalty
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that perfectly captures Michael's illogical loyalty and inappropriate comparisons

jokes.text: Our company is worth nothing. That's the difference between you and I. Business isn't about money to me, David. If tomorrow my company goes under, I will just start another paper company. And then another and another and another. I have no shortage of company names.
jokes.setup: Michael's passionate speech about business philosophy
jokes.punchline: Ends with the trivial point about having lots of company names
jokes.explanation: Great Michael speech that starts profound but ends with typical Michael priorities - having good company names

jokes.text: That's one of 'em!
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he has many company names
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates with 'Michael Scott Paper Company'
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback that shows Michael's 'creative' naming just involves putting his name first

jokes.text: Our balls are in your court.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to say 'the ball is in your court'
jokes.punchline: Mangles it into inappropriate plural version
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism that makes the phrase inappropriate and memorable

jokes.text: The day he is born. The day he grows hair. The day he starts a business. And the day he sells that business back to Dunder Mifflin.
jokes.setup: Michael listing life's defining moments
jokes.punchline: Includes growing hair as a major life milestone
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's odd priorities and personal insecurities about hair
